notes: Quantified axis with multiple anchors (7% royalty → $7M → $2.8M EBITDA → 12x multiple → ~$34-36M → net ~$22-23M). All actors named (Alex Hormozi), conditions explicit (restaurant franchise, 12x multiple, 30%+ rollover). Falsifiable (numbers are tied to specific assumptions and comparisons). Type label implicit but clear. Strong boundary statements acknowledge variability.

Named speaker (Alex Hormozi), resolvable conditions (paid social, direct-response), load-bearing claim (challenges production-quality dogma), falsifiable (top performers tracked across campaigns). Type label present (assertion). Bounded by caveat that high-production ads also win; mechanism implicit but understood.

Named speaker (Alex Hormozi), condition clear (substantive avatar change), mechanism provided (different avatars have different LTVs/close rates). Load-bearing (behavioral advice to not abandon during relearn period). Falsifiable (can measure if metrics re-baseline). Type label (assertion) present.

Named speaker (Alex Hormozi), mechanism explicit (capacity expands under load; discomfort = adaptation signal). Type label present (mechanism). Load-bearing (reframes growth pain as necessary cost). No ambiguous role-words. Falsifiable through observation. Two sources cited.

Named speaker (Alex Hormozi), operational definition of influence given (high likelihood of compliance). Load-bearing (defines a lever for supply constraints). Type label (assertion) present. Resolvable referents. Falsifiable (compliance rates measurable). Mechanism implicit but sound.

Named speaker (Alex Hormozi), explicit conditions (network effects must be genuine; ROIC must exceed alternatives). Type label (conditional) present. Mechanism provided (marginal user adds value to all users). Load-bearing (justifies distribution sacrifice under specific conditions). Bounded statements acknowledge false positives in founder claims. Falsifiable.

Named speaker (Alex Hormozi), mechanism explicit (culture = aggregate of reinforced behaviors; leader controls reinforcement). Type label (mechanism) present. Conditions listed (sales manager exists, owner not present daily, etc.). Load-bearing (attribution of inconsistency to management, not reps). Falsifiable. Three sources cited.

Named speaker (Alex Hormozi), mechanism explicit (most variations fail; winners found only through volume; better creative expands TAM non-linearly). Type label (mechanism) present. Extensive conditions listed (24 specific conditions). Multiple bounded statements acknowledge edge cases (quality floor, labor cost, creative infrastructure). Falsifiable. Ten sources cited. Old Spice analogy illustrative.

Named speaker (Alex Hormozi), mechanism explicit (vagueness amplifies via imagination; specificity forces comparison to survivable outcomes). Type label (mechanism) present. Conditions stated (functionally healthy in developed country). Load-bearing (actionable reframing of fear). Bounded by caveats re: untreated addiction/mental illness. Falsifiable. Five sources cited.

Named speaker (Alex Hormozi), mechanism explicit (free wrappers lower perceived risk, increasing conversion for cold-audience prospects). Type label (conditional) present. Nine conditions listed. Multiple bounded statements (digital products, cash flow constraints, quality threshold, impulse buying). Load-bearing (strategy for audience-building phase). Falsifiable. Three sources cited.

Named speaker (Alex Hormozi), operational definition provided (tolerance = duration of behavior maintenance under stress, not absence of pain). Type label (assertion) present. Conditions clear (tasks disliked/uncomfortable, no immediate reward). Load-bearing (defines a trainable component of mental toughness). Bounded by clarification that tolerance ≠ pain immunity. Falsifiable. Three sources cited.

Named speaker (Alex Hormozi), mechanism explicit (script validation → all variance is compliance; compliance trained via repetition). Type label (assertion) present. Extensive conditions (19 specific conditions including script validation, high-volume context, coachability). Multiple bounded statements (applies to scripted roles; does not apply to enterprise/consultative sales). Load-bearing (reframes training gap as solvable). Falsifiable. Eight sources cited.
